measurement_note: Size is K, the number of input trees. Each tier is a sequence of two-node BSTs whose roots merge into one strictly increasing right chain. The reference indexes roots once and visits every resulting node once. A correct slower implementation linearly searches the remaining root list at every mergeable leaf, completes all tiers, and fails by its quadratic root-search work.

cases.name: benchmark: merge 32 trees into a right chain
benchmark: merge 128 trees into a right chain
benchmark: merge 512 trees into a right chain
